archive_data.question_text: Who among the following is a celebrated singer of Chhath Puja songs?

explanation.english: Sharda Sinha is a famous folk singer from Bihar renowned for singing Chhath Puja songs.
explanation.tanglish: Sharda Sinha Bihar pattanathukku sonthamaana paadakiyar, Chaath Pooja paadalgal paaduvadharku prasiddhamanaavargal.

archive_data.question_text: The arteries divide into smaller vessels. On reaching the tissues, they divide further into extremely thin tubes called ______.

explanation.english: Arteries divide into arterioles, which further divide into capillaries when they reach the tissues. Capillaries are extremely thin blood vessels where exchange of materials occurs.
explanation.tanglish: Arteries arterioles aaga piriyum, adhu tissues-ai serum bodhu capillaries aaga melliyadhaga piriyum. Capillaries migavum melliya raththa kuzhthaigal.

archive_data.question_text: The causative agent of the deadly disease Ebola is/are:

explanation.english: Ebola is a deadly disease caused by the Ebola virus. It affects humans and other primates.
explanation.tanglish: Ebola virus naal earpaadhum payangara noi Ebola. Idhu manushyarkalaiyum matrum uyarnthottigalaiyum paridhu paduthum.

archive_data.question_text: Who among the following is set to become the first-ever female president of the Marylebone Cricket Club in its 233-year history?

explanation.english: Clare Connor became the first female president of the Marylebone Cricket Club (MCC) in 2021, marking the first time in the club's 233-year history.
explanation.tanglish: Clare Connor 2021-aam aandu Marylebone Cricket Club (MCC)-in 233 aandu varalarhil mudhalamaaga penn thalaivaraha theroappeddaar.

archive_data.question_text: In which year was the Indira Gandhi Institute of Development Research (IGIDR) inaugurated?

explanation.english: The Indira Gandhi Institute of Development Research (IGIDR) was established and inaugurated in 1987.
explanation.tanglish: Indira Gandhi Institute of Development Research (IGIDR) 1987-aam aandu arumbaiyakkappattadhu.

archive_data.question_text: ______ is played on a synthetic hard court.

explanation.english: The Australian Open is played on synthetic hard courts. Wimbledon is played on grass and French Open is played on clay courts.
explanation.tanglish: Australian Open sinthatic hard court medaiyil vilayattappadum. Wimbledon pull medaiyilum, French Open mannu medaiyilum nadakedum.

archive_data.question_text: The Khilafat movement was led by ______.

explanation.english: The Khilafat movement was led by the Ali brothers - Muhammad Ali and Shaukat Ali, along with other leaders.
explanation.tanglish: Khilafat iyakkam Ali Annanmar endru ariyappedum Muhammad Ali matrum Shaukat Ali aal alavarchiyinaal nadathappattadhu.

archive_data.question_text: In which year was the NITI Aayog formed?

explanation.english: NITI Aayog (National Institution for Transforming India) was formed on January 1, 2015, replacing the Planning Commission.
explanation.tanglish: NITI Aayog (National Institution for Transforming India) Janavari 1, 2015-aam aandu Planning Commission-ku pathilaha uruvakkappattadhu.

archive_data.question_text: Which of the following is the largest jute producing state in India?

explanation.english: West Bengal is the largest producer of jute in India, accounting for the majority of the country's jute production.
explanation.tanglish: Indiaavil ennikaiyaaga saanam (jute) uruvakkuum state West Bengal, idhu indiaavin mudhalaan saanam uruvakkum thotathaiyum adangiyadhu.

archive_data.question_text: Fatehpur Sikri was founded as the capital of the Mughal Empire by ______.

explanation.english: Fatehpur Sikri was founded as the capital of the Mughal Empire by Emperor Akbar in 1571.
explanation.tanglish: Fatehpur Sikri samraajyathin thalaavagam Akbar aalagaikarathinaal 1571-aam aandu uruvakkappattadhu.

archive_data.question_text: Which of the following Articles of the Constitution of India provides the 'Right to Education'?

explanation.english: Article 21A of the Indian Constitution provides the Right to Education as a fundamental right for children aged 6-14 years.
explanation.tanglish: India arasangathin Article 21A 6-14 vayathu kuzhanthaigalukku kalvi urimaiyai moola urimaiyahu kooruhiradhu.
